regions.us-ports: Today in Miami, winds are shifting from light WNW at 3 knots to a southerly breeze around 14 knots, with thunderstorms expected despite only a 16% rain probability. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, Port Everglades and Port Canaveral will also experience southerly winds near 13 knots accompanied by thunderstorms, with a 28% rain probability that may affect shore excursions and embarkation processes. By day three, the wind shifts to NNW at 10 knots, bringing drizzle and a 46% chance of rain, which could impact outdoor activities in Miami and nearby ports. Passengers and crew should monitor conditions closely for possible adjustments to scheduling and tender operations during this period.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, conditions in Nassau and neighboring ports like Freeport and Bimini feature light southerly winds at 6 knots with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions. Beyond 48 hours, we anticipate continued breezy conditions from the NNW, which may influence sea states around Puerto Plata and La Romana, though rain chances remain moderate.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, winds are southeast at 6 knots with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, providing favorable conditions for embarkation and shore excursions in San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Croix.
